Ad Text:
Senior Delivery Excellence Specialist | Perth CBD | Permanent Contract





We're seeking a Senior Delivery Excellence Specialist role in our Technology team to sharpen how programs and projects are delivered - driving insight, momentum and measurable improvement across the entire delivery lifecycle. Provide deep performance insights, lead improvement initiatives and help refresh our delivery management framework.

Your Role in Action

· Lead the refresh of our delivery management framework—creating practical, easy-to-use guidelines and templates that balance agile ways of working within a waterfall environment

· Actively engage stakeholders across the business to gather feedback and co-design templates that are genuinely fit for purpose and add real value

· Map and connect delivery processes with critical business workflows, ensuring the framework guides teams to involve the right people at the right time

· Drive core program and project delivery disciplines, including resource management, governance, reporting, methodologies and performance analysis

· Provide clear, insightful oversight of program and project health by monitoring delivery status, analysing performance and maintaining accurate, meaningful reporting

· Design, strengthen and embed governance frameworks and processes that enable consistent, high-quality program and project delivery

· Champion the adoption of best-practice program and project management principles, ensuring alignment with governance frameworks, systems and controls

· Identify and implement practical improvements that enhance cost efficiency, delivery timelines and quality—while providing trusted advice, mentoring and support to delivery teams

What Makes You a Great Fit

· Demonstrated expertise across portfolio, program and project delivery, with the ability to adapt your approach to suit different environments and levels of complexity

· A strong track record of building trusted, productive relationships with stakeholders at all levels of the organisation

· Proven experience designing, improving and embedding effective program and project management frameworks that genuinely support delivery teams

· Clear, confident communication skills—written and verbal—paired with the ability to influence, guide and bring people along on the journey

· The ability to move comfortably between detail and strategy, connecting day-to-day delivery with the bigger picture and communicating accordingly

· Tertiary qualifications in Project or Program Management, Business or a related discipline

By joining Western Power, you will contribute to powering a cleaner energy future. We are dedicated to fostering career development and promoting a healthy work-life balance, empowering you to drive innovation as we develop a network that will serve the Western Australian community for generations. You will also benefit from a range of perks and opportunities, including:

· Over 50% of our employment opportunities are driven by career progression.

· Flexible work arrangements to support part time work, working hours and working from home arrangements.

· The opportunity to purchase up to four weeks of additional leave per year.

· Access to salary packaging, social club activities, and discounted health insurance and gym membership.

· An award-winning employee recognition and benefits programme.

· An innovative team culture that's enabling the transition to renewable energy and decarbonisation.

· In addition to standard leave, enjoy three wellness leave days each year.

· Access to end-of-trip facilities at our Perth office and South Metro Depot, perfect for cyclists.

· On-site café available for convenient meals and refreshments throughout the day.

· 22 weeks paid parental leave and 5 days of First Nations cultural leave to support family and cultural connections.

Applications close on 19 May 2026

We value diversity and inclusivity, encouraging applications from all backgrounds, including women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, and LGBTQIA+ communities. As a WORK180-endorsed employer for women, we are proud of our commitment to inclusion. You can learn more about this through our Reconciliation Action Plan and Disability Access and Inclusion Plan.

Learn more about Western Power here: About (westernpower.com.au)

About the Role

D2i Consulting is seeking a highly capable Senior Data Engineer / Data Modeller to join an exciting engagement focused on building a modern Databricks lakehouse platform.

The role will commence with an initial 3–4 week Discovery phase, working closely with stakeholders to assess current state, validate assumptions, and define a fixed price delivery scope. This will be followed by a short mobilisation period (1–2 weeks) and transition into full delivery.

This is a hands-on role requiring strong technical capability combined with the ability to engage stakeholders and shape scalable data solutions.

Australian work rights are required for this position.

Key Responsibilities

Discovery Phase (Initial 3–4 weeks)

Assess current data landscape, architecture, and source systems

Validate data availability, quality, and source-to-target mappings

Analyse modelling complexity and define approach (e.g. canonical / CIM-style modelling)

Develop a structured delivery plan, including risks, dependencies, and sequencing

Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to align on scope and outcomes

Delivery Phase (Post Discovery)

Design and implement scalable Databricks lakehouse architecture (Bronze / Silver / Gold layers)

Develop logical and physical data models aligned to business and domain requirements

Build ingestion pipelines and data structures to support downstream analytics and modelling

Implement data validation, lineage, and governance practices

Produce high-quality documentation and support knowledge transfer

Skills & Experience

Strong experience as a Senior Data Engineer and/or Data Modeller

Minimum 3-4 years of experience as a Senior Data Engineer/Data Modeller is required

Hands-on expertise with Databricks (Lakehouse architecture, Delta Lake, PySpark, SQL)

3-4 years of hands-on Databricks experience is required

Experience designing data models and canonical data structures (CIM/CDM experience highly regarded)

Proven experience building data pipelines and ingestion frameworks

Strong understanding of data architecture, governance, and lineage

Ability to operate in discovery and ambiguous environments and shape delivery outcomes

Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Highly Regarded

Experience in utilities, energy, or asset-heavy industrie

Experience working on data strategy / discovery engagements leading into delivery

Knowledge of spatial or operational datasets (geoJSON, asset models, etc.)

What's on Offer

Opportunity to shape an evolving data platform

Work with a high-performing consulting team and enterprise client

Clear pathway from Discovery → Delivery

Flexible working arrangements

Careers with Impact

More than one million Australians trust us with their money, and that trust shapes how we work every day. Our members sit at the centre of everything we do, guiding our decisions and the impact we strive to make.

We're looking for people who are purpose-driven and accountable, who value inclusion and collaboration, and who are proactive in managing risk to protect our members' long-term outcomes. In return, you'll be part of an organisation where your work contributes to strong returns, real-world impact, and a culture that reflects the communities our members serve.





Bring your authentic and passionate self to this exceptional role #careerswithimpact


Join HESTA's internal Fixed Income team and play a pivotal role in shaping the credit strategy of one of Australia's leading superannuation funds. This is an opportunity to do meaningful work — managing portfolios that deliver real retirement outcomes for HESTA members.


• Deliver fundamental credit research across corporates, banks, and structured products — initiating coverage, conducting periodic reviews, and driving trade recommendations.
• Maintain internal credit scores and shadow ratings, underpinned by detailed financial models and sector analysis.
• Contribute to portfolio management strategy across internally managed Fixed Income, Enhanced Cash, and Domestic Private Debt portfolios.
• Support the team's right of veto on domestic private credit transactions through rigorous due diligence.
• Help implement and develop HESTA's ESG and Responsible Investment policies within the fixed income portfolio.
• Build and manage external market relationships to source new insights and stay across credit market thematics.

About You

• Demonstrated credit analyst experience of Australian corporate credit at a super fund, fund manager, investment bank or research house
• Strong financial modelling and credit risk analytics skills
• Experience analysing debt security issuers and conducting relative value assessment
• Track record delivering to deadlines across multiple stakeholders
• Rigorous analytical thinker with sharp attention to detail
• Motivated by outcomes and continuous improvement
• Strong communicator who can present complex credit views clearly
• Comfortable in a collaborative, values-driven team environment
• Undergraduate and postgraduate qualifications in accounting, commerce, or economics
• Deep knowledge of defensive asset classes including credit, debt, and cash
• Familiarity with Australian superannuation, investment governance, and industry fund culture


Benefits that matter and make a difference for our employees


• Leave for those moments that matter, an additional 6 days of leave at the end of year, up to 6 days paid volunteer leave, gender neutral paid parental leave of 20 weeks, Gender Affirmation leave, reproductive health and wellbeing leave, Cultural and Ceremonial leave. Access your LSL after 3 years, take AL at half pay, and purchase up to 2 weeks additional leave (just to name a few!).
• Your Professional development matters, up to $5k per year professional development and up to 8 days professional development leave, HESTA scholarships and free access to a range of premium learning tools.
• Your health and wellbeing matters, free annual flu shots and skin checks, incredible social events throughout the year and a comprehensive employee assistance program available 24/7.
• Your financial wellbeing matters, up to 15% super, financial planning support, end of year payment for all Enterprise Agreement-covered employees, incentivised Employee Referral Program and novated lease options.

We celebrate, value and include people of all backgrounds, genders, identities, cultures and abilities. We welcome and support applications from First Nations people, physically, neuro or culturally diverse, LGBTQI+, and people of any age.

We are proud to be WGEA accredited as an Employer of Choice for Gender Equity.


We want all candidates to feel safe, included and provided with the best opportunity to thrive, if you require reasonable adjustments during your application or throughout the recruitment process, please reach out to a member of the Talent team car••••@hesta.com.au and we'll call you to discuss.

ServiceStream is seeking an experienced Senior Finance Business Partner to join our central Group FP&A team. Reporting to the Group FP&A Manager, this role plays a critical part in enabling strong commercial outcomes by partnering closely with senior leaders across GBIS and the broader Group.

This is an opportunity for a commercially minded finance professional who enjoys working at the intersection of financial insight, strategy, and stakeholder engagement in a complex, national organisation.

This is a full time, permanent position based out of our Collins Street office supporting hybrid working arrangements.

About the role

Partner with executive and senior leaders to deliver high‑quality financial insight and decision support

Lead monthly P&L reporting, including detailed variance analysis, commentary, and performance metrics

Support end‑to‑end rolling forecasts and the annual budgeting process, providing insights into cost drivers, trends, and workforce utilisation

Prepare recharges, journals, and balance sheet reconciliations accurately and on time

Develop and embed meaningful KPIs to improve operational performance and efficiency

Support investment analysis and business cases to drive optimal commercial outcomes

Drive continuous improvement across reporting frameworks, systems, and processes, including TM1, SAC, and SAP S/4HANA

About you

You are a proactive and consultative finance professional with strong business acumen and the confidence to influence at executive levels.

Tertiary qualifications in Business, Commerce or Accounting

CA or CPA qualification

Strong experience in management reporting, forecasting, and commercial analysis

Advanced analytical skills, including Excel and BI tools (Power BI experience preferred)

Experience with planning and reporting systems such as TM1 and/or SAC (highly regarded)

A continuous improvement mindset and strong attention to detail

Excellent communication skills and the ability to build trusted stakeholder relationships

About us

Service Stream is an equal opportunity ASX‑listed business that develops and operates Australia’s essential services networks across telecommunications, utilities, transport, defence and social infrastructure industries. We keep communities connected.

Benefits of working with us include

Flexible working – genuine hybrid working options available

Growth – career development opportunities within a national, growing organisation

Discounts – access to retail partners such as Woolworths, Airbnb, JB Hi‑Fi and Bupa

Paid leave – parental, cultural, community service, study, volunteering and purchased leave

Culture – a supportive, inclusive workplace that values diversity and celebrates excellence

How to apply

Please submit your application via the Apply button, where you’ll be directed to create a profile in our system.

We welcome applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability or neurodivergence, members of the LGBTIQA+ community, Veterans, and individuals from all diverse backgrounds.

Applications will not be accepted via email. If you require reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact tal•••••••••••••@servicestream.com.au.

Company Description

At Colliers, we are enterprising. What sets us apart is not what we do, but how we do it. Our people are passionate, take personal responsibility and always do what’s right for our clients, people and communities. We accelerate their success by empowering them to think and act differently to drive exceptional results. 

Job Description

We are seeking an aspiring, technically competent commercial  Analyst to join our industry leading National Industrial Capital Markets team. Based in the Sydney CBD, this role supports the senior agents in analysing, marketing and  sale of industrial properties across the Australian market.

You will be actively involved in the campaign life cycle, researching the industrial market, data collection, and providing market intelligence to support the compilation of submissions and production of information memorandums.

The scope of the role will include:

Financial modelling & investment materials – build cash flow models (single assets + portfolios) and support submissions, pitches and information memorandums.
Development feasibility analysis – run feasibilities for major industrial development sites.
Due diligence support – assist agents with financial/technical due diligence during campaigns (incl. lease reviews and outgoings benchmarking).
Market & data intelligence – research industrial market insights and manage key team datasets (sales, valuations, operating agreements and sector data).

 

Qualifications

Ideally the right person for the role will be newly qualified Commercial Valuer, Assistant Development Manager or Analyst within a commercial property business. 

Other requirements:

University Degree Qualified & Real Estate License/Certificate
Intermediate with Microsoft Office (PowerPoint, Word, Excel and MS-Project).
Competent experience in Forbury Modelling
Excellent Communication skills
Understanding of local industrial property market and current trends

If the above resonates with you, and you want to accelerate your career within a market leading team, please call Tom Keady on 9257 0314

Additional Information

We foster an environment in which everyone at Colliers feels respected and comfortable bringing their authentic selves to do their best work. It is our people who set us apart and we actively recruit people who represent the diversity of the communities in which we operate. We take pride in doing what is right and operating in ways that benefit the business, our people, our clients, our communities, and the environment.

Please be advised that applications will only be accepted directly rather than via recruitment agencies.

Job description:



At Stockland, we are focussed on delivering meaningful change that improves how we work and how we serve our customers and communities. We're seeking a Change Delivery Lead to join our Customer and Digital function on a permanent basis, based in Sydney. This role plays a critical part in enabling successful business improvement and digital transformation outcomes across the enterprise.

We're Looking For a…

Reporting to the Head of Business Improvement and Innovation, this role leads change strategy and delivery across a portfolio of priority initiatives. You'll partner with project leads, leaders and subject matter experts to ensure change is right-sized and delivered to enable business growth.

What You'll Do

Lead delivery of lean, outcome-based change strategies across multiple business improvement and digital initiatives
Manage a portfolio of change activity to align messaging, reduce change fatigue and support effective adoption
Partner with leaders and project teams to assess impacts, risks and readiness for change
Design and deliver stakeholder engagement, communications and training approaches that support understanding and uptake
Leverage technology to aid delivery and embedment of portfolio initiatives

This is a permanent role, based in Sydney, within the Customer and Digital portfolio.

About You

You are an experienced change professional who enjoys working across fast-paced, growth-focussed environments. You bring a practical, people‑centred approach to change, and are confident engaging stakeholders at all levels. You are motivated by seeing change land well and make a real difference to how organisations operate.

Experience & Skills That Count

Proven experience delivering change across portfolio or complex programs of work
Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability, including senior leaders
Experience managing resistance to change and supporting adoption of new ways of working
Digital mindset and practical fluency with technology and AI-enabled tools
Background in project or transformation delivery
Relevant tertiary qualifications or change management certifications

The Right Mindset

Collaborative and relationship focused
Adaptable, resilient and comfortable with ambiguity
Accountable and outcomes driven

The Stockland Proposition

We're a community: a supportive, empowered team that works as one to create better places and experiences for everyone, including ourselves. We're committed to building an inclusive workplace where diverse perspectives are welcomed, and everyone feels a genuine sense of belonging.

We're committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. If you need adjustments during the recruitment process, just let us know.

Apply Today

Ad Text:
At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

The Australian Government has announced the stabilisation of existing business registers and their transfer from the Australian Taxation Office (ATO) back to ASIC, under the Registry Connect (RC) Program. This program aims to deliver reliable, secure, trusted, and efficient registry services to support the economy for the benefit of all Australians.

 

Sydney or Melbourne location
24-month fixed term contract
Salary from $152,074 to $163,970 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % superannuation
Applications will close at 11:59pm AEST, Tuesday 12 May 2026

 

The team 

The Digital, Data & Technology (DD&T) Portfolio Office provides a comprehensive range of services across portfolio management, project delivery, business planning, and governance to support ASIC's strategic priorities. We work closely in partnership with ASIC Technology business units and the newly established RegistryConnect (RC) program to ensure initiatives are aligned with both strategic objectives and operational requirements.

 

The role

As a Senior Business Analysts within the RegistryConnect Program, you will employ diverse business analysis techniques, including agile methodologies, to deliver high-quality project outcomes within established timelines. Your key responsibilities will encompass eliciting, analysing and managing both high-level and detailed business requirements, conducting enterprise analysis, assessing and validating solutions and addressing both functional and non-functional requirements.

More specifically, you will: 

Collaborate with business SMEs and Technology teams to analyse and validate business needs through stakeholder engagement and workshops.
Document business, functional and non-functional requirements in clear, accessible formats using user stories for Agile projects.
Design user interfaces aligned with User Centred Design (UCD) principles and Australian Government accessibility and usability standards.
Develop process maps, including current state analysis and future-state design.
Rapidly understand business structures and strategies to align work with organisational goals.
Support test planning and execution activities.
Assist with UAT preparation, system implementation and development of user guides and training materials.
Demonstrate strong self management and accountability in delivering team and organisational priorities.

 

About you

The ideal candidate this role will be an experienced, proactive and detail-oriented Business Analyst professional who possess strong analytical and communication skills. The ideal candidate will excel at translating complex business needs into clear, actionable requirements and thrive working within a collaborative environment with both business and IT stakeholders. They should also have a strong background in business analysis techniques, including Agile methodologies, Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) and be adept at delivering high-quality project outcomes within established timelines.

In addition, you will have:

Relevant tertiary qualifications or professional certifications, with Agile and SAFe certification being highly desirable.
Excellent knowledge of SDLC and Agile methodology.
Strong skills in eliciting, documenting, and managing requirements.
Experienced with industry process modelling and requirements management tools.
Demonstrated technology-based project experience within in large complex enterprise environments.
Exceptional analytical and enterprise-level problem-solving skills in IT systems and complex issue resolution.
Proven ability to independently manage multiple tasks or projects with full accountability for outcomes.
Skilled in delivering professional verbal and written communication to diverse audiences, with a strong commitment to customer service excellence.
Demonstrable experience in integrating different business applications and improving efficiency of workflows and processes.
Strong knowledge and previous experience working with systems and technology such as SAS, Microsoft Dynamics, Java, Siebel/Oracle fusion, Peoplesoft and Data Warehouse, highly desirable.
An existing Australian Government security clearance (Baseline/NV1) is highly desirable, however candidates without a current clearance will also be considered.

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications will close at 11:59pm AEST, Tuesday 12 May 2026.

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms. 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent are encouraged to apply. 

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

Click here to view ASIC's salary and benefits guide.

To read more about ASIC, you can visit our website or review our Corporate Plan.

To work with us, you need to be an Australian citizen and be prepared to complete an ASIC Suitability and Baseline Assessment.

ACCIONA is a global company, leading in the development of regenerative infrastructure that creates a positive impact on society. Our workforce consists of more than 41,500 professionals, present in more than 40 countries across the five continents, all contributing in our mission to design a better planet. Are you a passionate individual who wants to make a difference, promote sustainable development, and find solutions to the biggest global challenges, including climate change, overpopulation, and water scarcity? Come and join us in building the infrastructure our planet needs to achieve a sustainable future.

The Opportunity

This role plays a key part in driving continuous improvement, strengthening internal controls, and supporting change management initiatives across the ICT function. You will be responsible for identifying and remediating control gaps, enhancing and redesigning processes, contributing to the annual audit program, and supporting effective stakeholder engagement throughout change activities.

A key focus of the role is to track, prioritise, and manage improvement opportunities to ensure ongoing operational uplift. Reporting to the ICT Performance and Improvement Manager, you will help champion operational excellence and support the delivery of a high‑performing, well‑governed ICT function.

Key responsibilities:


Lead the assessment, documentation, and remediation of internal control gaps, maintaining a robust ICT controls framework and reporting progress using data‑driven insights.
Drive process improvement initiatives by analysing data, benchmarking performance, and prioritising optimisation opportunities across the ICT function.
Manage the internal audit program end‑to‑end, including annual planning, execution, documentation of findings, follow‑up actions, and coordination with external auditors.
Redesign ICT processes to improve quality and efficiency, ensuring high‑quality process documentation (process maps, RACIs, SOPs) and alignment with industry best practice and ACCIONA standards.
Support effective change management and stakeholder engagement by assessing change impacts, developing high‑level change plans

About You:

Strong background in compliance, audit, and process improvement (ideally with professional services/Big 4 experience), with demonstrated success leading change and engaging stakeholders.
Excellent communication and leadership skills, with proven ability to design, document, and embed new or redesigned processes.
Deep experience with internal control frameworks, gap assessment methodologies, and continuous improvement practices.
Bachelor's degree in IT, Business, or related field, with preferred certifications in ITIL, Agile, Change Management, or Internal Audit, and 8+ years' experience in ICT operations or performance improvement.

AEMO at the Heart of Energy   

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role 

The Operations division is responsible for operating today’s systems and market securely and reliably, along with supporting AEMO to navigate the energy future. Within the Market Management department of the Operations division, sits the WA Prudentials and Settlements team which is responsible for the sound financial management of the Western Australian Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM). The functions include settlement, prudential management, metering operations. The role level is flexible based upon the applicant’s experience. 

Working autonomously, you will use your well-developed analytical skills and subject matter expertise to identify, analyse, define, test and implement rule, process and system changes that improve the efficiency and effectiveness of operational processes and systems. Working towards becoming a subject matter expert, you will contribute to strategic projects related to market enhancement and process improvement projects through the provision of analysis, option development and recommendations on solutions. 

This role can be based in Perth or Sydney. 

What you will be doing  

Working independently or in small groups within the team to perform metering and settlement functions in compliance with the ESM Rules and WEM Procedures within defined deadlines.

Identify and undertake root cause analysis, reporting, risk assessment and implementation of fixes or mitigations related to technical or compliance issues.

Develop and maintain open, supportive working relationships with team members and other teams, establish effective networks across the business and contribute to agreed team outcomes.

Using an understanding of systems, rules and processes, undertake complex quantitative and qualitative analysis which assess market outcomes to inform decision making and support projects.

Recommend process and procedure improvement activities and projects, that reduce compliance risk, improve operational efficiency and deliver improved market outcomes. 

What you will bring to the role  

Degree in economics, computing, commerce, engineering, science or a related discipline.

Experience in a commercial or regulatory environment. Including interpreting and operating in compliance with technical documents and/or legal instruments, such as rules and regulations.

Balanced Methodology: You are comfortable working across various delivery frameworks. You excel in environments that require thoughtful documentation and upfront planning alongside active delivery.
The "Analyst's Toolkit": Expert proficiency in process modeling, data interrogation, and translating complex business needs into actionable functional requirements.
Collaborative Engagement: A natural ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders, from technical digital teams to operational site managers.
Self-Directed & Proactive: A proven track record of operating independently, taking initiative, and driving outcomes without the need for constant oversight.
Personal Attributes

Analytical Depth: You look beyond the surface to understand the "why" and ensure the "how" is sustainable.
Energetic & Positive: You bring a "can-do" attitude and a commitment to fostering a supportive, high-performing team culture.
Self-Starter: You are naturally curious and take the lead in identifying and solving problems within your scope.

Excellent temporary role until the end of 2026 for an experienced data migration specialist
ASAP start through until end of 2026
Located in Albion
AO6 level role
Our client, a Queensland State Government Department, is seeking an experienced Data Migration & Testing Lead to play a critical role in the delivery of a major SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation. This role supports frontline services by ensuring the accuracy, integrity and reliability of data during large‑scale ERP transition activities.


Working collaboratively with project teams and business stakeholders, you will lead data migration governance and testing activities to deliver high‑quality, compliant outcomes that align with organisational and operational needs.


Key responsibilities
As the Data Migration & Testing Lead, you will:

Develop and manage testing strategies, plans, schedules and reporting to ensure quality outcomes across data migration and system testing activities.
Provide specialist advice, knowledge transfer and training to stakeholders to support system implementation, testing and ongoing support.
Oversee the end‑to‑end data migration process, ensuring secure, accurate and validated transfer of data into the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment.
Develop and execute data migration testing activities, including test plans, test cases and validation processes to confirm data accuracy, completeness and reliability.
Record, analyse, prioritise and manage defects and issues, supporting effective resolution and transparent reporting to key stakeholders.
Collaborate closely with the Project Manager, technical teams and business users to coordinate testing and migration readiness and meet delivery milestones.
Engage business units to understand data requirements, facilitate workshops, communicate progress and support change impacts.
Ensure all migration and testing activities comply with organisational, regulatory, security and governance requirements, supporting audit and reporting needs.
Highly desirable requirements

Proven experience managing data migration and validation for large‑scale ERP or financial system transitions.
Strong knowledge of data cleansing, data mapping and data transformation methodologies.
Demonstrated experience coordinating and supporting User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
Qualifications in Information Technology, Data Management or a related field.
Experience in SAP ECC6 to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud migrations or equivalent ERP transformation programs.
To be considered for this role, please forward your cover letter and your resume in WORD format quoting reference number ARDMTL to app••@windsor-group.com.au or contact Annette Rafter on 07 •••• •001.

face2face reference: 670385

Influence national higher education pricing and funding decisions

Kompozition is a forward-thinking Australian company helping organisations design and deliver complex systems more efficiently. We use a unique, leading-edge digital engineering platform based on the behaviour modelling language (BML) to help our clients understand and solve big, complex problems.

About the Role

The Senior Engineer will play a key role in supporting clients to model complex systems using the Kompozition Platform. You will help clients understand and improve their operations, build high-quality models, and translate complex information into actionable engineering artefacts.

This role is ideal for a technically minded professional with 10+ years of engineering experience who enjoys working with customers, facilitating workshops, and applying systems thinking in fast-paced environments. Defence or Federal Government experience is highly desirable.

What We're Looking For

We’re looking for someone who enjoys solving problems, working with people, and learning new tools and ideas. If you enjoy helping others succeed, can quickly pick up technical concepts, and want to make a real impact, this could be the role for you.

We welcome people from all walks of life—including those new to the tech, government or Defence industry. Our ideal team member is:

An individual who has strong observation, analytical and problem-solving skills
A strong communicator who enjoys working with others
Organised, reliable, and able to manage their time well
Comfortable learning new tools and explaining them to others
Curious and always looking to improve
Happy to work onsite with clients (mostly in Canberra)

Bonus if you have

Familiarity with systems thinking, modelling tools such as Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) tools, or process mapping
An interest in technology, design, or systems engineering
Previous Defence or Government experience
Experience in customer support, consulting, or analysis roles

What You'll Do \ Key Responsibilities

Client Engagement & Support

Onboard, train, and enable client teams in the Kompozition platform.
Facilitate workshops to help clients capture intent, define objectives, and structure information.
Support clients in developing, validating, and maintaining system models.
Collaborate with Customer Success and Kompozition’s internal teams to ensure client adoption, satisfaction, and retention.
Act as the client voice internally by sharing insights with Kompozition’s internal teams.
Track and report client progress against agreed outcomes.

Digital Engineering & Modelling

Create digital system models and generate artefacts such as needs, requirements, tests, and workflows.
Apply systems thinking to understand complex interfaces, data flows, and operational scenarios.
Analyse technical documentation to derive requirements and identify key system behaviours.
Establish and maintain traceability from business goals to engineering artefacts.
Solve complex modelling challenges and contribute to improving modelling methods and platform capabilities.

Consulting & Technical Leadership

Lead requirements analysis, business process reviews, and system definition activities.
Represent requirements using diagrams, scenarios, or prototypes where appropriate.
Conduct impact analysis, manage requirement changes, and maintain documentation quality.
Support client engineering, testing, and development teams by clarifying requirements and ensuring alignment.
Contribute to developing internal knowledge bases, playbooks, and training materials

Essential Requirements

Australian Citizenship (due to government & Defence-related work)
The successful candidate must hold, or be eligible to obtain, an Australian Government Security Vetting Agency (AGSVA) security clearance
Qualifications and/or experience in a STEM-related field (e.g. Engineering, IT/ Programming, Technical Analysis)

BAT is evolving at pace into a global multi-category business. Our purpose is to create A Better Tomorrow™ by Building a Smokeless World. To achieve our ambition, we are looking for colleagues who are ready to join us on this journey! Tomorrow can’t wait, let’s shape it together!

British American Tobacco has an exciting opportunity for an Area Strategic Planning Manager in Sydney!

This role is responsible for driving regional demand planning by analyzing market performance, sales patterns, and customer demand to ensure accurate forecasting. It strengthens planning governance across the APMEA South Area, delivers high‑quality documentation, and provides actionable insights to senior leadership. By enabling data‑driven decision‑making, the position helps shape financial strategy, supports long‑term growth, and gives to the company’s overall value creation.


Your key responsibilities will include:

Above Market Demand Planning

Drive Planning & Information processes including strategic plans and scenario analysis at the business unit level.

Provide challenge and guidance on demand forecasts submitted by local markets.

Analyze IMS, SHP & OFF trends on consistent basis for relevant end markets.

Regularly analyze market and sales trends across key end markets.

Incorporating market sensitivities in coordination with End Markets and leads multiple priorities and deadlines across diverse markets.

Conduct risk analysis, sensitivity testing, and benchmarking.

Collaborate with internal teams to monitor financial targets, improve return on investment, and perform forecasting.


Preparing & Reporting Monthly Activities

Analyzing high level summaries of market performance, summarizing monthly positions for actuals.

Analyze industry positions and report trends and movements by coordinating with the End Markets.


Management of Planning cycle reporting

Prepare documentation and reporting materials for planning cycles, and presentations and analysis for leadership and finance reviews.

Partner with operations, marketing, regulatory affairs, and strategy teams.


Planning Data Repository:

Maintain robust Area dashboards to support decision-making.


Regional Submissions and Meeting Minutes:

Review and validate inputs for as well as compile Meeting Minutes and Updates for the upcoming planning sessions.


Ad hoc and Analysis Requests Reporting:

Deliver Reports and Analysis requested by region and other partners on ad hoc basis.


Strategic & People Leadership Capabilities

Align forecasts with regional/global goals, anticipate industry shifts, and drive strategic decisions beyond reporting.

Act as a trusted business partner, fostering collaboration, accountability, and developing high‑performing, teams with varied strengths.


What are we looking for?

Bachelor’s degree in commerce (Economics, Finance, Business Analytics, Accounting) or related field.

Professional qualification preferred (e.g., CFA or equivalent experience, CPA, ACCA); MBA is an advantage.

Minimum 5 years of Planning and/or Finance experience, preferably in a multinational or regulated industry.

Experience in leading Planning and Information operations across multiple countries.

Strong business partnering and excellent communication skills.

Full working rights with no restriction.

What we offer you?

We offer a market leading annual performance bonus (subject to eligibility)

Flexible Benefits via LiveWell Platform including an annual lifestyle spending account available for you to spend on what matters most to you - while making benefits relevant, personalized, and easy to access.

Half day wellbeing leave per month.

Mobile phone allowance.

Ad Text:
Company description:



Insignia Financial Limited is an Australian financial services company which offers a range of products and services including financial advice, superannuation, investment management and trustee services



Job description:



Senior Risk and Governance Specialist

Make risk real and help us make better decisions
Put your fingerprints on the future to progress your career
Embed risk that enables safe and trusted acceleration of Technology

The opportunity to join our team

We're looking for a Senior Risk and Governance Specialist to join our 1st Line Technology Risk team and play a key role in shaping how technology and cloud risk is managed across Insignia Financial. In this role you'll partner closely with a key business unit as its applications transition into and operate within cloud environments, helping to identify, assess and manage technology risks while helping teams make confident, well-informed decisions.

You'll work closely with teams who are actively building and operating in the cloud, gaining exposure to multiple cloud technologies and cross-functional teams.

The cloud environment is still maturing, which means your work will have real impact. You'll help shape how technology risk is managed in a cloud-first organisation, guiding teams through new challenges and strengthening controls as the environment evolves. On top of that, you'll be part of a close, collaborative team that values support, open communication, and a bit of fun along the way.

This is a hands-on, first line technology risk role giving you the opportunity to influence how risk is managed as the organisation continues its cloud-first journey.

Key Responsibilities:

Act as the first-line Technology Risk partner to a dedicated technology business unit
Support risk identification, assessment and remediation across cloud-hosted applications
Provide pragmatic guidance on technology and cloud risk matters
Work closely with cloud platform teams
Manage and track technology incidents, issues and remediation actions
Contribute to technology risk uplift initiatives and governance improvements

What you will bring

You're someone who understands technology risk but is equally comfortable engaging with technical teams. You enjoy working closely with stakeholders, asking the right questions, and helping teams think through risk in a practical way rather than simply applying frameworks.

Strong experience in first-line Technology Risk
Exposure to cloud environments, ideally AWS (Azure or GCP also valuable)
Ability to engage confidently with technical teams and stakeholders
Understanding of technology risk frameworks and governance practices
Knowledge of the Australian regulatory landscape (APRA CPS 234, CPS 230 etc.)
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Find the better way

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people feel good about their money as one of Australia's leading financial wellbeing organisations. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we'll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives.

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. With us, you'll do work that builds your technical know-how and challenges our entire industry to move forward. Along the way, you'll be free to explore new ideas and technology, solve problems in a team, and independently to get great things done. Around here, we like to call it "putting our fingerprints on the future".

People-inspired tech

Deep expertise combined with creativity can take on the trickiest of problems. Join our team of brilliant minds and mentors as we explore our way to innovation and apply technology in more human ways than ever.

To Apply

Read more here about why you should join our team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.

Temple & Webster is Australia’s #1 online destination for the home. We’re a tech-led retailer, a logistics powerhouse, and a team of creative problem-solvers.  We don’t just move boxes; we build the infrastructure that allows millions of Australians to live more beautifully.  We are ambitious, we are fast-paced, and we are obsessed with delivering a seamless customer experience.

The Role
We aren't looking for a "maintainer" - we’re looking for an architect of scale.  As the Head of Supply Chain Technology, you will own the technical engine that drives our most complex operations.  You will sit at the intersection of physical logistics and digital engineering, transforming our ERP from a system of record into a strategic competitive advantage.


This is your chance to lead high-stakes digital transformation in a business where the supply chain is the very heart of the customer journey.
Why this role will excite you
True ERP Solution Ownership: You define the architecture and roadmap for our ERP to ensure it supports a scaling retail giant.
Bridge the "One Tech" Divide: No more siloed departments. You’ll be the primary liaison between Supply Chain and the broader Engineering teams, synchronizing delivery methodologies and representing the function on the Change Advisory Board (CAB).
High-Stakes Innovation: From 3PL visibility and SaaS integration to AI-driven planning, you’ll have the mandate to explore emerging tech that solves real-world bottlenecks.
Commercial Empowerment: You’ll own the SCT budget and P&L, giving you the autonomy to drive cost-governance and reinvest savings into high-impact innovation.
Leadership that Matters: You’ll mentor a specialised team, fostering a culture of technical excellence, continuous improvement, and professional growth.
Who You Are
The "Translator": You speak fluent "Logistics" and "Engineering." You can take a complex operational pain point and turn it into a clean, scalable technical specification.
ERP Veteran: You’ve managed and scaled Enterprise platforms within a high-volume e-commerce, retail, or distribution environment.
Systems Thinker: You look at the supply chain end-to-end - from supplier origin to the customer’s doorstep - and you see exactly where the data needs to flow better.
Strategically Grounded: You enjoy high-level strategic planning but are equally comfortable diving into the details of ISO-aligned risk frameworks or vendor negotiations to get the best outcome.
Why T&W?
The Environment: A stunning HQ in St Peters with a hybrid rhythm with 2 days WFH.
The Culture: We take our work seriously, but not ourselves. Enjoy daily mindfulness, catered team lunches, and a collaborative energy that encourages fresh ideas.
The Rewards: Competitive salary, paid birthday leave, generous parental leave, and a significant staff discount to help you live more beautifully.
The Impact: You’ll be a key player in an ASX-listed company that still moves with the agility and spirit of a startup.
Our supply chain is the backbone of our success. Are you ready to build the tech that powers it?

Location: Tweed Heads, Lismore & Far North Coast NSW (Hybrid)
Ad Text:
About the Role

This isn’t your typical customer service role — it’s where empathy meets commercial insight.

As our Customer Experience, Resolution & Insight Executive, you’ll own the end-to-end customer resolution process — managing escalations, driving service recovery, and uncovering insights that shape how Alpha Car Hire shows up for customers across digital, call centre, and branch operations.

Seeking a Senior Business Analyst with experience using DevOPs and user-centric design and Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG).

Job details

As a  Business Analyst within the Government Services Branch, you will play a critical role in analysing enhancements and defining business rules and user requirements for the application. You will provide specialist business analysis expertise while collaborating closely with application developers, the Product Manager, Product Owner, Scrum Master, test analysts and designers to deliver high‑quality, functional products through iterative and incremental delivery. 

 The Business Analyst will be responsible for creating, maintaining and updating  related documentation and artefacts, ensuring user requirements are effectively translated into working solutions. A key responsibility includes providing decision‑making support to the Product Manager, enabling a strong focus on the product goal and roadmap. 

 The Business Analysts will support agile ways of working by fostering collaboration and delivering value to users at every sprint. They will operate under limited direction, contribute to project planning and scheduling, and support the achievement of outcomes. The roles require the demonstration of sound judgement, initiative and effective decision‑making. 

Key duties and responsibilities

Collaborate with business and technical teams to identify and analyse issues, define business rules, and develop requirements. 
Map current/future state processes (BPMN). 
Create user stories, functional/non-functional specifications, and user journeys. 
Evaluate system functionality, conduct gap analysis, and translate business needs into clear, traceable requirements for developers. 
Act as a central liaison between teams to ensure solutions are fit for purpose. 
Assist with the creation and prioritisation of backlog items, including developing acceptance criteria. 
Agile environment - Engage in an iterative and incremental project lifecycle and adapt to changing requirements and priorities throughout the development process.  
Focus on light weight documentation. 
Upskill/mentor junior staff. 
Embrace change and be adaptable to evolving requirements, with the ability to reprioritise and adjust focus in response to customer feedback and shifting business needs.  
Encourage frequent and continuous customer collaboration throughout the development process, through regular feedback sessions and demonstrations.  
Emphasises and models collaboration and teamwork, often working closely with cross-functional teams (incl. developers, testers, and other stakeholders).  
Prioritises delivering minimum viable products (MVPs) or increments of value in short, fixed time frames (Sprints). 
Highly Desirable Criteria 

3-5+ years of relevant experience.  

Desirable Criteria 

Experience working and aligning to Agile ways of working. 
Experience using DevOPs. 
Experience working in Government settings.  
Knowledge of user-centric design and Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG).  
Security Required: Baseline  Security Clearance required

The Organisation

Homelessness Australia is a national peak body representing the homelessness sector and advocating for people without homes. 

Working collaboratively with homelessness services, lived experts, governments, researchers, and our 700+ members and 5,500+ engaged supporters, Homelessness Australia is focused on driving evidence-based practice and shifting systems, attitudes and policies to strengthen Australia’s response to homelessness.

With new funding in place, Homelessness Australia is scaling a small yet high-performance team to advance their mission at pace and scale through increased advocacy, policy and practice leadership, and research capability, 

The Opportunity

Reporting to the CEO, this new role will lead sector engagement, policy development and advocacy to shape and advance Australia’s homelessness policy agenda.

Bringing together sector knowledge, lived experience, the latest research and international best practice, you will develop compelling, evidence-based policy positions, while mobilising stakeholders across government, community and academia to progress reform priorities.

The role will also lead national advocacy initiatives, coordinate major events including Homelessness Week, and build the relationships and coalitions required to strengthen the sector’s collective voice.

The Right Fit

We are seeking an influential policy leader with deep expertise in homelessness and a strong track record in advocacy and sector engagement. 

Energised by purpose-led work, you bring a sophisticated understanding of the systems that contribute to homelessness and the leadership capability to navigate complex stakeholder environments, mobilise the sector, build coalitions and drive coordinated advocacy that delivers tangible outcomes and alignment across diverse groups.

Equally important is your ability to translate evidence into compelling narratives, producing high-quality policy papers, submissions and communications tailored to government, sector and public audiences.

Essential capabilities to ensure impact include:
Policy Leadership & Development – Proven ability to lead policy development processes that integrate sector knowledge, lived experience, research and best practice to produce influential, evidence-based policy proposals.
Advocacy & Sector Mobilisation – Demonstrated experience establishing and leading advocacy campaigns, building networks and mobilising stakeholders to achieve policy outcomes and systemic change.
Stakeholder Engagement & Relationship Building – Highly developed relationship management skills, with the ability to build and maintain partnerships across government, homelessness and First Nations services, academia and lived experience communities.
Written & Strategic Communication – Exceptional writing capability, with experience producing policy papers, submissions and communications tailored to a range of audiences including government, sector and media.
Research & Insight Generation – Experience initiating or contributing to research that informs policy reform, including the ability to translate data and insights into actionable recommendations.
Leadership & Influence – Ability to lead complex processes, inspire collaboration and influence decision-makers to shift policy, systems and public understanding.
The Benefits

This is a rare opportunity to have an impact and strengthen homelessness systems to reduce the incidence and impact of homelessness. You will leave a legacy and build trusted relationships across complex stakeholder environments, while maintaining a focus on delivering outputs that are practical, accessible and valued by the sector.

If successful, you will enjoy: 
Working and collaborating with talented, engaged and creative people to shape policy outcomes 
Developing and engaging a small support team to deliver impact at pace and scale 
Collaborating with the sector, government, lived expert and research communities to drive collective impact
Flexible working arrangements, a maximum term contract to June 2028 and funded interstate travel

If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours

We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.

How you'll make an impact

As a Senior Portfolio Analyst, you'll play a pivotal role in enabling AMP's enterprise-wide transformation by delivering actionable insights that drive strategic alignment, portfolio optimisation and value delivery. You'll be responsible for analysing and reporting on portfolio performance, supporting integrated planning and ensuring benefits realisation across complex change initiatives. Your technical expertise will be central to the rollout and optimisation of Atlassian cloud tools (JIRA, Confluence), where you'll configure, maintain and enhance dashboards, data models and reporting frameworks. You'll champion data hygiene and governance standards, ensuring the integrity of portfolio data and enabling agile decision-making. By partnering with delivery teams, finance and transformation leads, you'll help AMP navigate change with clarity, precision and measurable impact.

What you will being to the role

Bringing with you experience in business analysis, enterprise PMO or transformational delivery functions, your technical toolkit includes deep familiarity with agile environments, portfolio management principles and hands-on experience with Atlassian tools, SharePoint and MS Teams. You're skilled in developing and maintaining integrated dashboards, tracking value delivery and managing complex data landscapes. Your commercial acumen allows you to interpret financials, build business cases and support strategic planning with confidence. With a growth mindset, resilience and the ability to influence senior stakeholders, you'll help AMP deliver smarter, faster and with greater transparency.

You'll thrive here if…

If you can adapt from BAU to the ambiguous with ease, you'll do well here. Change is never easy, so bring your commitment, grit and growth mindset.

Because we run lean, you'll be expected to jump in and deliver across a variety of areas. Meaning, you'll be closer to the action and executive decisions that influence where we go next.

If you're someone that can hold their own, you'll find AMP quite liberating.

Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.

We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website

Ready to create your tomorrow?

If you're someone who sees opportunity where others see challenge, come and work with us in smart, progressive ways as we transform an iconic Australian brand for the future. And, through a series of career-defining moments, create your own tomorrow.

Don't procrastinate! We review applications when we get them, and if we discover the ideal candidate, we may close the role earlier than the advertised close date.

Looking forward to meeting you.

Head of IT & Digital Transformation

Sydney, NSW | Permanent | Full‑time | Hybrid

Heavy Motor Insurance Australia (HMIA) is entering a major phase of technology and digital transformation and we’re looking for a senior technology leader to shape and lead it.




As Head of IT & Digital Transformation, you will define and drive HMIA’s technology strategy while ensuring complex initiatives are delivered successfully, adopted effectively, and deliver real business value. This is a hands‑on role with executive visibility and genuine influence across the organisation.




About HMIA

Heavy Motor Insurance Australia (HMIA) is a specialist underwriting agency focused solely on the heavy motor industry. With deep industry expertise, we manage underwriting and claims in‑house and work closely with brokers and partners to deliver practical, efficient and trusted outcomes. Our business is built on strong governance, operational excellence and a clear appetite for smart, well‑managed transformation.




What you’ll do

Reporting to the Chief Operating Officer, you’ll be responsible for both technology strategy and delivery, ensuring initiatives align to HMIA’s business objectives, risk appetite and governance frameworks. Your role will include:

Define and lead HMIA’s IT and Digital Strategy, translating business priorities into a clear, actionable roadmap

Act as a trusted technology advisor to the Executive Team, providing strategic insight and practical recommendations

Lead the selection and implementation of core underwriting, claims and policy administration systems, including complex data migrations

Govern and deliver AI, automation and digital initiatives to drive operational efficiency and improved customer outcomes

Establish and maintain strong governance across architecture, security, risk, delivery and vendor management

Oversee HMIA’s cloud, Microsoft 365 and SharePoint environments to ensure performance, security, adoption and cost effectiveness

Partner with business leaders to define requirements, manage change and ensure technology solutions are embedded and delivering value

Build, mentor and lead a high‑performing technology function with clear accountability and capability uplift




What you’ll bring

You’re a strategic but pragmatic technology leader who is comfortable owning outcomes in a regulated and risk‑sensitive environment. You’ll bring:

7+ years’ experience in senior IT leadership or technology transformation roles

Proven experience defining and delivering enterprise‑wide technology strategies

A strong track record leading complex system implementations and large‑scale data migrations

Deep knowledge of Microsoft technologies including Azure, Microsoft 365, SharePoint, security and data platforms

Strong vendor and stakeholder management capability, with confidence engaging executive and non‑technical audiences

Experience operating within regulated or risk‑focused environments

An ability to balance strategic vision with hands‑on delivery and governance

A clear, structured communication style and comfort operating in ambiguity and competing priorities

Commercial awareness and a strong focus on measurable outcomes

Experience within insurance or financial services, exposure to underwriting and claims systems, and experience governing AI and automation initiatives will be highly regarded.




Why HMIA?

Hybrid working that actually works

Leadership that backs ideas, trusts experts and supports growth

A specialist business where tech isn’t an afterthought — it’s a true enabler

Real opportunity to shape systems, strategy and outcomes, not just maintain them




Living Our Values

At HMIA, our values guide how we work and how we lead. We’re looking for someone who naturally operates with:

Transparency – clear, honest and trusted decision‑making

Efficiency – purposeful delivery with strong governance

Smart thinking – curiosity, learning and informed judgment

Reliability – ownership, consistency and follow‑through

Agility – adaptability in a changing environment

Strength – confidence in our people, systems and decisions




If you’re a senior technology leader who wants to shape the future of HMIA and see your strategy delivered in practice, we’d love to hear from you.

Company Description


Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: 8th May 2026

Job Description


Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

You will lead the portfolio management of the internal Australian Fixed Income portfolio to ensure it delivers its risk and return objectives in the context of Rest’s broader investment beliefs and whole of fund requirements. You will enhance the investment process with a focus on quantitative and qualitative research, data quality, ESG integration, portfolio construction, risk management and efficiency.

Key accountabilities:

Lead a team of one Senior Analyst and support the development of other Fixed Income and Debt team members.
Contribute to Whole of Fund investment thinking by working collaboratively with the broader Fixed Income & Debt team, Listed Assets, Asset Allocation team, Responsible Investments team and Governance team.
Back up and support the management of the internal Cash portfolio.
Represent the Australian internal Fixed Team when meeting with internal and external stakeholders.
Take primary responsibility for the investment strategy, portfolio construction and day‑to‑day management of the internal Australian fixed income portfolio to deliver its risk and return objectives.
Lead fundamental and quantitative research on interest rate and credit markets, including macroeconomic, valuation and technical drivers.
Ensure full adherence to Rest’s regulatory requirements, internal policies and mandate guidelines.  

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in finance or related discipline
Ideally post-graduate qualifications e.g. Masters, CFA

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

Demonstrated, relevant experience with an excellent track record in managing Australian fixed income portfolios in either investment management and/or superannuation
Extensive knowledge and understanding of global fixed income markets, macroeconomic analysis, portfolio theory and designing investment solutions.
Extensive experience in portfolio management and trading fixed income and cash investments across physical and derivative markets.
